mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll growth signals aggregate income growth which drives consumption and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward tests show non-positive 20-day SPX returns when PAYEMS is rising over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds three percent reflecting restrictive monetary policy, equity valuations face downward pressure from higher discount rates and tighter credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, or the spread between returns above and below threshold is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment creates a recessionary demand-supply signal that depresses earnings expectations and risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is declining and UNRATE is rising, S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, strength in retail sales triggers expectations of monetary tightening that outweighs the direct demand benefit, causing negative twenty-day equity returns as discount rates reprice higher.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between RSAFS monthly changes and 20-day SPX returns persists (Rank IC > 0.03) when CPIAUCSL > 240 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum captures real-time supply-side economic acceleration that translates into immediate earnings surprises, driving positive five-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below twelve hundred thousand signal collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, generating negative twenty-day equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST < 1200, or the difference vs HOUST >= 1200 is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of falling federal funds rates and rising consumer prices creates a stagflationary signal where monetary easing is insufficient to offset inflationary margin compression, resulting in negative sixty-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of FEDFUNDS decline and CPIAUCSL increase fails to predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment breaches five percent reflecting full employment turning to labor market slack, demand destruction fears dominate investor positioning, pushing negative twenty-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered episodes when UNRATE > 5.0%, or forward returns are insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
